description_long: This object consists of two stylized deciduous trees, each with a rounded, bushy canopy and a straight, cylindrical trunk. One tree features vibrant green foliage, while the other displays a rich brown-orange color, suggesting seasonal variation such as summer and autumn. The canopies are composed of clustered, angular leaf shapes that give a textured, almost cartoon-like appearance. The trunks are slender and brown, with no visible branching below the canopy. The trees are positioned close together, with their canopies partially overlapping. No roots are visible, and the overall form is compact, making it suitable for placement in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: A pair of stylized deciduous trees with rounded, textured canopies—one green and one brown-orange—on slender trunks, suitable for urban landscaping.
description_view_0: Two trees with overlapping canopies, green tree in front and brown-orange tree behind, both with textured foliage.
description_view_1: Green tree on the left and brown-orange tree on the right, both canopies fully visible and separated, showing more of the trunks.
description_view_2: Brown-orange tree in the foreground, green tree behind, both canopies visible with clear separation and trunk alignment.
description_view_3: Brown-orange tree on the left, green tree on the right, both canopies and trunks visible, with small colored blocks on the ground.
